predictions.description: Bridge bottleneck survives (spectrum > 0.50, warm > 0.30 all conditions)
Temperature 0.5 increases Jaccard by 0.05-0.15 vs baseline
Temperature 0.9 decreases Jaccard but all models > 0.10
5-waypoint paths show higher bridge frequency than 9-waypoint
Control pair (stapler-monsoon) remains unstructured across all conditions

claims.claim: Models have distinct, stable conceptual gaits
claims.statistic: Claude 0.578, GPT 0.258; 2.2x gap stable across 9,500+ runs
claims.qualification: Gait rank order not perfectly preserved across protocol conditions (Kendall W = 0.840)

claims.statistic: Mean asymmetry 0.811; 87% significant (p < 0.05); triangle inequality holds 91%
claims.qualification: Detectability resolution-dependent: falls below 0.60 threshold at 5 waypoints
Weakest robust claim; LLMs find routes between any concepts at 7 waypoints; single-pair validation inadequate

claims.claim: Bridge concepts are bottlenecks, not associations
claims.statistic: spectrum 1.00, metaphor 0.00, germination > plant; bridge freq most protocol-robust (>0.97)
claims.qualification: New-model cohort bridge freq CI includes zero (diff -0.100, CI [-0.286, 0.089])

claims.statistic: 12/16 family/model combos show monotonic decrease; 3/4 families perfect

claims.claim: Prediction accuracy degrades from characterization to mechanism
claims.qualification: Qualitative predictions succeed (~50%); quantitative predictions fail (~0%)

claims.claim: Bridge frequency is the most protocol-robust property
claims.statistic: >0.97 across all waypoint/temperature conditions; ANOVA interaction p=0.886

claims.claim: Gait rank ordering is largely stable across protocol conditions
claims.qualification: GPT and DeepSeek swap in some conditions

metrics.name: Gait (Intra-model Jaccard)
metrics.definition: Mean pairwise Jaccard similarity of waypoint sets across repeated runs of the same model on the same pair

metrics.name: Asymmetry Index
metrics.definition: 1 minus the mean cross-direction Jaccard (forward vs reverse waypoint sets)
metrics.interpretation: Higher = more asymmetric paths; 0 = perfectly symmetric

metrics.name: Bridge Frequency
metrics.definition: Fraction of runs in which the predicted bridge concept appears in the waypoint sequence

metrics.name: Waypoint Transitivity
metrics.definition: Mean Jaccard similarity between the union of A-B and B-C waypoint sets and the A-C waypoint set
metrics.interpretation: Higher = more compositional (A-C path reuses A-B and B-C concepts)

metrics.name: Positional Profile
metrics.definition: Bridge frequency at each waypoint position i in the sequence
metrics.interpretation: Reveals where bridges anchor in the navigational sequence

metrics.name: Triangle Inequality Excess
metrics.definition: d(A,B) + d(B,C) - d(A,C) where d = 1 - Jaccard
metrics.interpretation: Positive = triangle inequality holds; negative = violation

metrics.name: Pre-fill Displacement
metrics.definition: Change in bridge frequency when waypoint 1 is pre-filled vs unconstrained

phases.name: Pilot (Conceptual Gait)
phases.keyQuestion: Do models produce consistent conceptual paths?
phases.finding: 4 models show distinct, stable gait signatures (Jaccard 0.258-0.578)

phases.name: Reversals (Asymmetry)
phases.keyQuestion: Are A-to-B and B-to-A paths symmetric?

phases.name: Transitivity & Convergence
phases.finding: 4.9x transitivity over controls; triangle inequality holds 91%

phases.name: Cue Strength & Polysemy
phases.keyQuestion: Does bridge frequency track cue strength?
phases.finding: Monotonic gradient in 12/16 families; forced crossings highest

phases.name: Positional Profiles
phases.keyQuestion: Where do bridges anchor in the sequence?

phases.name: Pre-fill & Curvature
phases.keyQuestion: Can bridges be displaced by pre-filling?
phases.finding: Pre-fill disrupts bridges; cross-model distance metrics invalid (r=0.17)

phases.name: Salience & Normalization
phases.keyQuestion: Do competitor count or dominance predict fragility?
phases.finding: Competitor count irrelevant; gait normalization has zero effect

phases.name: Dominance & Facilitation
phases.finding: Dominance ratio fails; facilitation effect real but unpredictable

phases.name: Generality (8 models)
phases.keyQuestion: Do findings generalize to new architectures?
phases.finding: Gait, asymmetry, bridges replicate across 8 models; relation class matters

phases.name: Expanded Generality & Robustness
phases.finding: All findings replicate; bridge freq most robust (ANOVA p=0.886)

hypotheses.name: Gemini Gradient Blindness
hypotheses.predicted: Gemini zeros on >= 5/10 gradient pairs, <= 2/10 causal

hypotheses.name: Gait Normalization Rescues Distance Metrics
hypotheses.predicted: Cross-model r from ~0.17 to >0.50 after normalization

hypotheses.name: Pre-Fill Facilitation Crossover Regression
hypotheses.predicted: Negative slope CI excluding zero; crossover at 0.40-0.50
hypotheses.observed: Slope CI [-6.748, 0.723] includes zero; crossover at 0.790

hypotheses.name: Bridge Bottleneck Generalization to New Models
hypotheses.predicted: Bridge freq CI would include zero

Temperature 0.5 increases Jaccard by 0.05-0.15 vs baseline
Temperature 0.9 decreases Jaccard but all models > 0.10
5-waypoint paths show higher bridge frequency than 9-waypoint
Control pair (stapler-monsoon) remains unstructured across all conditions
